speaker: Jay Stratton
text: There really were religious fundamentalist extremists within the Pentagon that had a severe adversity to this topic. These are national security experts, senior members of the national intelligence community who are putting their religion above national security.
significance: Reveals that religious ideology among senior Pentagon officials actively impeded UAP investigation efforts, representing a previously undisclosed barrier to research.

speaker: Jay Stratton
text: A senior DOD official actually stopped me in the hallways in the Pentagon and told me that we were doing the devil's work.
significance: Provides a specific firsthand account of religious opposition to UAP research at senior levels of the Department of Defense.

speaker: Jay Stratton
text: I'm briefing about an advanced aerospace vehicle that has been detected on multiple sensors that's absolutely there, and you're telling me that you think these are demons and we shouldn't look at them and study them.
significance: Confirms multi-sensor detection of advanced aerospace vehicles while highlighting the irrational resistance faced by investigators presenting hard evidence.

speaker: Jay Stratton
text: The first choice was make peace with silence and sit on what is humanity's greatest secret, all the while keeping Americans in the dark.
significance: A former government official characterizes UAP information as 'humanity's greatest secret,' indicating the perceived magnitude of what is being withheld from the public.

speaker: Jay Stratton
text: I couldn't, nor would I ever reveal classified information, but I knew that if I could say just enough at the unclassified level to make the media aware and our government officials aware of the reality of the UAP topic, then Jay hopefully would be able to use that momentum to go ahead and achieve the goals that we set out to achieve inside the government.
significance: Reveals the deliberate strategy behind Lue Elizondo's public disclosure efforts - using unclassified information to generate momentum for internal government reform on UAP.

speaker: Christopher Mellon
text: How can any one organization, institution, religion, or government control that or censor that or gatekeep that? No one has the right to keep fundamental truths away from the American people and humanity.
significance: A powerful statement challenging the legitimacy of any authority to withhold information about non-human intelligence from humanity.

speaker: Christopher Mellon
text: Senator Rubio and Warner, to their great credit, put national security above their own personal political careers and took the risk of engaging publicly on this issue. When they began to engage, the Defense Department had to admit to the world that this was real and it was happening, and that immediately took the entire credibility of the issue up a huge notch.
significance: Mellon explains how Congressional engagement forced DoD acknowledgment and legitimized the UAP issue publicly.

speaker: Jay Stratton
text: There was one classified briefing that I gave to members of the House and Senate, where I took military pilots to tell them the story of their encounters with UAP. Those stories left them so rattled that they had trouble sleeping that night.
significance: A senior official reveals that classified UAP briefings with firsthand military pilot testimony had profound psychological impact on members of Congress, suggesting the information shared was deeply disturbing.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: The United States' most trusted military servicemen and women and irrefutable data from our intelligence collection systems have been observing UAP for decades.
significance: Elizondo asserts that UAP observations are backed by both credible military witnesses and intelligence system data over a long period.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: What we are seeing with UAP are vehicles that can make immediate right-angle turns, instantaneously accelerating and stopping on a dime at speeds up to ten times faster than the SR-71.
significance: Describes specific performance characteristics that far exceed known human aerospace capabilities, supporting claims of non-conventional technology.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: The human body can only take so much before the internal organs start to separate themselves from everything they're hooked to, which is usually bad. Even if it was a drone, it would rip the drone, the unmanned vehicle apart.
significance: Emphasizes that observed UAP maneuvers would be lethal to humans and destructive to known aircraft, implying exotic propulsion or non-human origin.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: UAP have been observed operating in space, in air and underwater, and they're moving seamlessly through each of these environments without any normal signature and without compromising performance.
significance: Describes the transmedium capability of UAP, indicating technology that defies conventional physics by operating across multiple domains without performance degradation.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: UAP seemed to be defying the natural effects of earth's gravity without any obvious means of doing so. No signs of propulsion or lift, meaning no wings, no control surfaces or ability to maneuver.
significance: Elizondo describes anti-gravity as a key observable characteristic of UAP, emphasizing the absence of conventional propulsion systems.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: There's a particular case of one of these objects maintaining a complete stationary position inside of 120 knot winds, essentially inside of a hurricane.
significance: Provides a specific military observation of UAP demonstrating seemingly impossible flight characteristics that defy known physics.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: There's actually a six observable, which is not a flight characteristic, that is biological effects.
significance: Elizondo reveals a sixth observable characteristic of UAP beyond the commonly discussed five flight capabilities, indicating these phenomena have measurable physiological impacts on humans.

speaker: Garry Nolan
text: They had data of military personnel, intelligence officials and others associated with the Department of Defense who had direct interactions with UAP. And because of that direct interaction suffered some kind of medical harm.
significance: Stanford professor confirms he was approached by CIA to study medical injuries from UAP encounters affecting military and intelligence personnel.

speaker: Garry Nolan
text: These people had scarring inside of their bodies and inside of their brains.
significance: Describes specific physical evidence of internal injuries from UAP encounters, suggesting these craft emit harmful energy fields.

speaker: Garry Nolan
text: The population had about a 25% mortality rate within seven years of having an interaction.
significance: Startling mortality statistic suggesting UAP encounters pose serious, potentially fatal health risks to those in close proximity.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: Meanwhile, Chris Mellon and I were working with the Senate behind the scenes to craft UAP legislation that was then taken by Marco Rubio and pushed into the coronavirus relief bill.
significance: Reveals the behind-the-scenes legislative strategy that successfully embedded UAP disclosure requirements into must-pass legislation.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: When President Trump signed this legislation into law, it then forced the Pentagon and the UAP task force to provide written reports to Congress, both classified and unclassified.
significance: Documents a pivotal moment when legal mandates for UAP reporting to Congress were established.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: Frankly, there are a lot more sightings than have been made public.
significance: Direct assertion from a former government insider that official UAP disclosure remains incomplete.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: As we continued to make progress, a very powerful disinformation campaign began. This effort was to destroy my reputation and credibility, full stop.
significance: Elizondo directly alleges an organized effort to discredit him as he advanced UAP disclosure efforts, suggesting institutional resistance to transparency.

speaker: Jay Stratton
text: When the government started running this despicable disinformation campaign against Lue, I actually received an email one day from the Pentagon Public Affairs office with this narrative that they were providing back to the media that was completely false. I replied all to that email and said, 'This is wrong,' and they ran with it anyway.
significance: A former government official provides firsthand testimony of witnessing Pentagon disinformation efforts and his unsuccessful attempt to correct the record.

speaker: Jay Stratton
text: They actually told the American people that Lue Elizondo did not work there, and that was an outright lie.
significance: Direct accusation from a government insider that the Pentagon deliberately lied to the public about Elizondo's employment in UAP programs.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: All you have in your life is really your credibility and your word.
significance: Elizondo reflects on the personal stakes of coming forward, emphasizing why attacks on his credibility are so damaging to whistleblowers.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: They don't want anybody that's based on facts being involved in this, so what do they do? They go online and try to belittle Elizondo and me and anybody else that's trying to do it in a scientific way.
significance: Alleges a coordinated effort to discredit those pursuing scientific investigation of UAP phenomena.

speaker: André Carson
text: There is something there measurable by multiple instruments, and yet it seems to move in directions that are inconsistent with what we know of physics or science, more broadly.
significance: A sitting Congressman officially acknowledges on record that UAPs demonstrate physics-defying capabilities measured by multiple sensor systems.

speaker: André Carson
text: I learned that the Legacy Program was quietly behind the scenes pushing back, and they were creating bureaucratic hurdles. They were somehow able to block the funding that was approved for the UAP task force.
significance: Direct accusation from a Congressman that a hidden 'Legacy Program' actively sabotaged congressionally-approved UAP investigation funding.

speaker: André Carson
text: There's this hidden hand that is blocking all of these actions and things I'm doing. A subversive government overriding the real government.
significance: Explosive statement from a member of Congress describing a shadow government actively undermining legitimate governmental UAP oversight efforts.

speaker: Christopher Mellon
text: The main players in the Legacy Program have long been the Central Intelligence Agency, the United States Air Force, the Department of Energy, and major defense contractors.
significance: Names the specific government agencies and entities allegedly involved in the UAP legacy program.

speaker: Christopher Mellon
text: The Deputy Director of Science and Technology at the CIA controls the UAP crash retrieval portfolio.
significance: Makes a specific claim about which CIA official allegedly oversees crash retrieval operations.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: We have intelligence teams quickly notified if there's some UAP event that might involve a crashed vehicle or pieces from a crashed vehicle. The United States Air Force uses their own special forces, but they also coordinate with other special forces in other branches of US military.
significance: Describes the multi-branch military coordination structure allegedly in place for rapid UAP crash retrieval operations.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: They're so well organized that they basically can get to a site, gather material, whatever it is, and once it's in their possession, they have the authority to classify it, and therefore make any information about it unavailable to the public, even admission that it exists unavailable to the public.
significance: Explains how classification authority is allegedly used immediately upon retrieval to deny even the existence of recovered UAP materials.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: They've used the security and classification language in the Atomic Energy Act to hide the Legacy crash retrieval program.
significance: Elizondo directly accuses officials of exploiting the DOE's unique classification system to conceal crash retrieval programs from oversight.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: I'm aware of at least two advanced non-human species, one of which made contact with the Legacy crash retrieval program. The other of which, the bodies were recovered by the crash retrieval program in various crashes.
significance: A specific claim from a former government insider about multiple non-human species and direct contact with a retrieval program.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: Another option that's being considered is that maybe some of these were not 'really crashes,' but that they were left here for us to examine in order to advance our technology forward at a faster pace.
significance: Introduces the provocative theory that crashed craft may be intentional technological 'gifts' from non-human intelligence.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: Some of this UAP activity we are seeing here in the United States may actually be a result of a Chinese UAP reverse engineering program.
significance: Suggests adversarial nations may have made progress in reverse engineering UAP technology, raising serious national security implications.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: If Xi Jinping had access to this, if Putin had access to this, and they thought that we did not have access to similar capabilities than what they did, do you think for a second that they wouldn't consider using it to achieve their ends of domination?
significance: Frames UAP reverse engineering as a critical geopolitical concern and potential threat to global power balance.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: Some presidents have been briefed on the basic facts about the situation, but they are not provided with all the details.
significance: Suggests that even U.S. presidents lack full access to UAP information, implying programs operate beyond executive oversight.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: The Americans, the Russians, and Chinese all have crash retrieved craft from some other civilization, and there's some belief at this point that maybe this should be revealed to the public.
significance: Elizondo describes a high-level Bush administration panel that allegedly confirmed multiple world powers possess crash-retrieved non-human craft.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: This is a topic that has a potential to create serious ontological shock to society. It's not just national security. It's psychological, it's sociological, it's theological and it's philosophical.
significance: Articulates the multi-dimensional nature of disclosure concerns beyond simple national security, explaining why secrecy has been maintained.

speaker: Jay Stratton
text: They look me in the eyes and said, 'Jay, we cannot tell you what we saw because the OSI, Air Force Office of Special Investigations, came in here, had us sign non-disclosure agreements.' 'We did see something, we did scramble on something, but we can't talk to you about it.'
significance: A government investigator describes being stonewalled by military personnel who were silenced by OSI non-disclosure agreements about a UAP intercept.

speaker: Jay Stratton
text: Historically, every time a military member had a UAP encounter, it was very quickly swept under the rug, and they were discouraged from talking about it.
significance: Confirms a systematic pattern of suppressing military UAP encounters and silencing witnesses.

speaker: Jay Stratton
text: There was a culture of silence. I didn't talk to anybody about it, not my wife, not my colleagues, not people I knew. They just shut up. There's ramifications if you were to divulge any of this information to anyone.
significance: Describes the pervasive fear-based culture that prevented UAP witnesses from discussing their experiences even with family.

speaker: Robert Jacobs
text: It shot a beam of light, and it hit the warhead, flew up like this, shot another beam of light down at the warhead, went like this, shot under the beam of light at the warhead, went under here, shot a beam of light at the warhead, and then flew out the way it had come in. All the while, everything in our frame was moving along at 8,000 to 9,000 miles an hour. There's nothing of ours that could've done that.
significance: First-hand account describing a UAP apparently targeting a dummy nuclear warhead with precision beam weapons at extreme speeds.

speaker: Robert Jacobs
text: Within the span of eight days, we lost 20 nuclear missiles to UFOs.
significance: Extraordinary claim that UFOs caused the failure of 20 nuclear missiles in just over a week, suggesting advanced technological interference capabilities.

speaker: Robert Jacobs
text: Vandenberg is home to the infrastructure that supports our National Missile Defense project, the system that defends our country from incoming ballistic missiles and nuclear weapons. So it's one of the most important bases we have.
significance: Establishes the critical national security context for UAP incursions at this specific military installation.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: If you do something within the government, eventually it can be accessed by Freedom of Information Act. But if you do something through contractors, they're not available through Freedom of Information Act. I had been involved myself in projects where we made sure that the material was under contractor labels so they couldn't be accessed easily by the public.
significance: First-hand admission of deliberately using contractors to shield UAP-related projects from public transparency laws.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: The real risk in transferring technology that is not useful to us today to a corporate entity. Over decades, that corporate entity comes to basically possess and control access to it for their own purposes, not for the purposes of national security.
significance: Describes how exotic technology transferred to contractors becomes privatized and removed from government oversight over time.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: Non-human intelligence is not government information, and the government should not be keeping that knowledge from the people.
significance: A direct statement asserting that knowledge of NHI existence belongs to the public, not to government secrecy programs.

speaker: Hal Puthoff
text: This energy field would completely isolate the craft from the environment. So you might ask then, how does the craft actually move forward within its bubble? The craft inside the bubble has a way of altering the bubble's properties in order to change its direction of motion.
significance: Puthoff describes a theoretical propulsion mechanism involving energy field manipulation, suggesting advanced physics concepts that could explain UAP flight characteristics.

speaker: Hal Puthoff
text: UAPs have exhibited propulsive performance characteristics that imply the generation of 1,100 billion watts of power. This is more than 100 times the daily electrical utility power generated in the US.
significance: Provides a specific, quantified claim about UAP energy requirements that demonstrates capabilities far beyond human technology.

speaker: Hal Puthoff
text: Once you've realized that space and time are being engineered to be different inside the craft than they are outside the craft, then these anomalies that people report that sound completely absurd can actually make sense.
significance: Offers a theoretical framework from a credentialed physicist that could explain multiple reported UAP anomalies including missing time and impossible interior dimensions.

speaker: Hal Puthoff
text: We also observe transmedium travel because the craft is moving within its own space-time, and the outside environment through which it's moving is inconsequential. This would allow a craft to go seamlessly from space through the atmosphere into the water.
significance: Provides a physics-based explanation for one of the five observables that has been repeatedly documented by military witnesses.

speaker: Christopher Mellon
text: If we can understand the technology that we're observing, it has so many potentially beneficial impacts, including clean energy. We're seeing these craft doing unbelievable things, and it's not combustion engineering.
significance: Mellon articulates the potential transformative benefits of understanding UAP technology, specifically noting it operates on principles beyond conventional combustion-based propulsion.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: This is technology that could replace oil, gas and coal and protect our planet for future generations.
significance: Elizondo makes a direct claim about the revolutionary energy potential of recovered UAP technology.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: You could put a nuclear bomb inside one of these things and let it fly right through a building, through concrete, through steel, wherever you want it, anywhere in the world.
significance: Describes the catastrophic weapons potential of UAP technology if acquired by adversaries, explaining national security concerns around disclosure.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: I would opt for making any new energy source a humanitarian, uh, issue. That it should be open. And we know how to do that. We've done that with nuclear power.
significance: Elizondo advocates for treating UAP-derived energy technology as a humanitarian resource to be shared openly, comparing it to nuclear power regulation.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: We have people that have very high clearances both today and in the past, who did really important work for our government or continue to do important work for the government, who have come forward with some claims about the US having in the past recovered exotic materials, and then we reverse engineered those materials to make advances in our own defenses and technologies.
significance: Elizondo confirms that credentialed insiders have made claims about recovered exotic materials and reverse engineering programs to him directly.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: When I have a whistleblower coming forward, telling me that they're in fear of their life and that people have been hurt protecting and hiding this information, that gives me reason for concern.
significance: Elizondo reveals that whistleblowers have told him people have been harmed to protect UAP secrets, suggesting extreme measures to maintain secrecy.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: They have worked on craft of out of this world origin, but until... they leak a video before they commit suicide by shooting themselves in the back of the head four times, um, I just don't know that that's going to get out.
significance: A darkly sardonic statement suggesting whistleblowers face lethal consequences, implying the program is protected by threat of death.

speaker: Tim Burchett
text: Last year, House Intelligence Committee held a hearing on UAPs. They brought in some Pentagon bureaucrats who only had two answers to the questions they were asked. 'I don't know,' or 'That's classified.'
significance: Congressman directly criticizes Pentagon officials for stonewalling Congress with non-answers during previous UAP hearings.

speaker: Tim Burchett
text: This was a tremendously proud moment for my colleagues and myself to watch the fire that we started continue to grow.
significance: Indicates Congressional momentum and commitment to continued UAP transparency efforts beyond a single hearing.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: Since mankind first started developing atomic energy, there has been consistent UAP activity at sites involved with the atomic and nuclear process.
significance: Elizondo directly links UAP interest to humanity's nuclear capabilities, reinforcing a pattern reported across decades of sightings at nuclear facilities.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: We have evolved more technically in the last 80 years than we have in the previous 200,000 years. And the uptick in UAP activity has increased in line with our advancement of harnessing energy.
significance: Suggests a correlation between human technological advancement and increased UAP monitoring, implying intentional observation by non-human intelligence.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: Despite developing massively new technologies, we don't see an equivalent moral increase in how we deal with each other.
significance: Reflects on the disparity between technological and ethical human progress, potentially explaining why UAP phenomena may be concerning to observers.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: We've got a big mess on our hands. We've got 80 years of lies and deception that quite frankly ruined a lot of people's lives, and nobody wants to take responsibility for that.
significance: Elizondo directly characterizes the UAP cover-up as 80 years of lies that caused real harm to individuals.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: This is not an endeavor here to go out and punish anyone. But nonetheless, we would need to know what they learnt, taxpayers paid for this. And I think offering amnesty is extremely important.
significance: Elizondo advocates for amnesty as a practical approach to disclosure, emphasizing taxpayer rights to knowledge over retribution.

speaker: Luis Elizondo
text: But where there was smoke, there was a fire, and Congress is now aware of it. And so what that allows us to do is circle the wagons and try again.
significance: Confirms that despite legislative setbacks, Congress has been made aware of substantive evidence warranting continued pursuit of disclosure.

speaker: Nancy Mace
text: There are certain individuals who didn't want this hearing to happen because they feared what might be disclosed.
significance: A sitting congresswoman publicly acknowledges active resistance to UAP disclosure efforts within the government.

speaker: Hal Puthoff
text: I am unbelievably hopeful that it's going to filter down into our society to where everyone realizes that the human species is actually in a melting pot of many species throughout the galaxy.
significance: A senior scientist who worked on classified government programs expresses his belief that humanity exists among multiple galactic species.

speaker: Hal Puthoff
text: The people behind the Legacy Program truly believe that the secret is so intense that they can't share it with the American people. There may be aspects of this that are so intense they can't share it. However, I think the basic fact that we are not alone in the universe, we can share that with the American people.
significance: Puthoff acknowledges the existence of a 'Legacy Program' while arguing for partial disclosure of the basic non-human intelligence reality.

speaker: Hal Puthoff
text: We are still only just beginning the greatest paradigm shift in human history, the age of disclosure.
significance: A credentialed insider characterizes the current moment as the beginning of humanity's most significant paradigm shift.
